exercises.name: Superband Deadlift to Row
exercises.description:
Stand on a superband with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the band handles (or ends) with both hands. 

Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to lower into a deadlift. As you hinge downward, pull the band toward your torso in a row, driving your elbows backward and squeezing your shoulder blades together. 

Stand back up while keeping the band engaged, then immediately repeat in a continuous, controlled rhythm.

exercises.name: Superband Deadlift to Upright Row
exercises.description:
Stand on a superband with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the band handles (or ends) with both hands. 

Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to lower into a deadlift. As you stand back up, pull the band up toward your chin in an upright row, driving your elbows upward and outward. 

Lower the band back to starting position at your sides, then immediately repeat in a continuous, controlled rhythm.

exercises.name: SuperBand Dislocates
exercises.description:
1. Stand with your hands wider than shoulder-width apart with light tension on the band throughout the entire movement.

2. Reach your arms overhead, and back towards your hips.

3. Slowly return to the start position, in reverse.

4. You can grip the band narrower to make it more challenging.

exercises.name: SuperBand Face Pulls
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the band in both hands, pull the band up and towards your forehead.

3. Your upper arms should be parallel to the floor, with your hands pointed towards the ceiling. 

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet flat and knees bent. Loop the band around your waist, like a seat belt, and secure it beneath both feet. 

2) Execute the movement by driving through the heels, extending your hips upward and raising your glutes off of the ground.

3) Extend as high as possible, pausing for a moment at the top before returning to the start position to repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Glute Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start on all fours, with the band looped around one foot, and the hand on the same side. 

2. Fully extend the leg that is holding the band, squeezing the glute hard for a moment once the leg is straight. 

3. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Superband Good Morning
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright placing both feet inside the band loop, place the end loop of the band behind your neck.

2.Keeping your knees relatively straight, bend at the hips, reaching your hips backwards.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Half Kneeling Diagonal Chop
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground level to a stable object. Assume a half-kneeling position with the band positioned diagonally across your body, opposite the anchored end. 

Hold the band with both hands. Pull the band diagonally across your body in a chopping motion, from the anchored side toward your opposite shoulder, engaging your core throughout the movement. 

Return under control to the starting position and repeat. Switch sides after completing the set.

exercises.name: Superband High Row to Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground height to a stable object. Stand facing the anchor, holding the free ends of the band with both hands. 

Perform a high row by pulling the band toward your upper chest, elbows out and squeezing your shoulder blades together. 

Once the row is complete, follow immediately with a bicep curl by bending your elbows and bringing your hands toward your shoulders. Lower the band under control and repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Superband Hollow Hold to Knee Tuck
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband behind your head at ground level. Lie on your back and hold the free end of the band with both hands. 

Extend your legs and arms into a hollow hold position, keeping your lower back lightly pressed into the floor and core engaged. 

Pull your knees toward your chest while simultaneously bringing your arms forward against the band’s resistance, then extend back into the hollow hold.

exercises.name: Superband Knee Drives
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband to a stable object at ground height and loop the other end around your foot. Stand facing away from the anchor in an athletic stance. 

Drive the opposite knee up toward your chest explosively against the resistance of the band while keeping your core tight and torso upright. 

Lower the knee under control and repeat the movement continuously with the same leg.

exercises.name: SuperBand Kneeling Hip Thrust
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Place the band around your waist. Get into a kneeling position where you are seated on your lower legs.

3. Thrust your hips forward, coming up off of your legs, and flexing your glutes. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Lat Pull Down
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a seated position, grasp the band with both hands. 

3. Fully extend your arms up towards the anchor point, until your elbows are straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until your hands are next to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Superband Lateral Squat Walk
exercises.description:
1.Place both feet inside the resistance band, creating tension on both legs, hands pulling the band.

2.Take short steps sideward alternating your left and right foot.

3.After several steps, do just the opposite side bringing you back to where you started.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Low Row to Front Raise
exercises.description:
Anchor the superband at ground level and stand facing it. Hold the handles with both hands, arms extended toward the anchor. 

Pull the band toward your torso, driving your elbows back into a low row. After completing the row, stand tall and raise your arms up to shoulder height for a front raise before returning to the starting position.

exercises.name: SuperBand Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Get into a plank position on your palms, with your feet towards the anchor point. 

3. With the band wrapped around both feet, slowly bring one knee up towards your chest. 

4. Return to the start position before bringing the opposite knee in towards the chest. 

5. Continue alternating left and right sides.

exercises.name: Superband Overhead Lat Pull Apart
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the band overhead with hands shoulder-width apart.

2.Pull it apart, bringing the band behind your back, and arms parallel to the floor.

3. Return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet together, on the band, and the band in both hands. 

2. Press the band overhead. While maintaining this position, step one foot back into a lunge position. 

3. Push yourself up to return to the start position. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: SuperBand Overhead Squat
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet shoulder-width apart, with the band in both hands. 

2. Press the band directly overhead with both hands and maintain this position. 

3. With hands overhead, slowly sit back and down into a squat without leaning forwards. 

4. Stand back up to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band with feet shoulder-width apart. 

2. Take the band in both hands, press it directly overhead, then bend the elbows so that the hands come towards the upper back.

3. While keeping the elbows in this position, extend the hands upwards towards the ceiling until your arms are straight. Return to the start position under control and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Pallof Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the band in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the band directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. 

5. Return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: SuperBand Pallof Press and Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the band in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the band directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. With arms extended, bring your hands directly overhead.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: SuperBand Plank Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Get into a plank position on your palms.

3. With the band in one hand, reach towards the anchor point until your arm is completely straight.

4. Pull the band all the way in towards your ribs. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Superband Pull Apart
exercises.description:
1. Stand upright with arms extended straight out in front of you, holding the ends of the band with both hands.

2.Perform a reverse fly motion, moving your hands out towards your sides, keeping arms straight and parallel to the floor.

3.Return to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Pull Through
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. While facing away from the anchor point, ensure that the band goes in between your legs. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.

3. Holding the band in both hands, hinge at the hips, bringing the torso forwards and the hands back towards the anchor. 

4. Drive your hips forward to return to the standing start position.

exercises.name: SuperBand Push Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by looping the band around your upper back, and holding it in both hands.

2. Get into a plank position on the floor. Slowly lower your body towards the ground, then push yourself all the way back up to the start position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Quadraped Kick Back
exercises.description:
Anchor the superband at ground level in front of you and loop the other end around one foot. 

Get into a quadruped position facing the anchor, with hands and knees on the floor. Keep your core braced and back flat as you drive the banded foot straight back and slightly upward, extending your hip fully. 

Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position under control.

exercises.name: Superband Resisted Sit Ups
exercises.description: Anchor the band behind you. Hold it across your chest and perform a sit-up, keeping the band taut.

exercises.name: SuperBand Reverse Lunge and Press
exercises.alternateName: banded reverse lunge to shoulder press
exercises.description:
1. Start bt standing with feet together on the band, with the band in both hands at chest height. 

2. Step one foot back and descend into a reverse lunge. 

3. Return to the start position by driving through the front foot.

4. Press the band directly overhead until your elbows are straight. 

5. Bring the hands back down to chest height and repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Reverse Lunge to Singel Arm Row
exercises.description:
Anchor one end of a superband to a stable platform, bench, or similar object at about waist height. 

Hold the other end of the band in one hand, opposite your forward leg. Step your other foot back into a reverse lunge, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. 

While in the lunge position, perform a row by pulling the band toward your torso, driving your elbow backward and squeezing your shoulder blade. Push through your front heel to return to standing, then repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Rotational High Pull
exercises.description:
Anchor the superband at ground level to a stable platform. Stand in a split stance—one foot slightly forward, but not in a deep lunge. 

Hold the free end of the band with the hand opposite your forward leg, arm extended toward the anchor point. Initiate the movement by pulling the band upward and across your body, rotating your torso and hips until your chest faces sideways away from the anchor. 

Keep your elbow high at the top of the pull and engage your core to control the rotation. Slowly return to the starting position with control.

exercises.name: Superband Row to Face Pull
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground height to a stable object. Stand facing the anchor, holding the free ends of the band with both hands. 

Perform a row by pulling the band toward your torso, elbows close to your sides and squeezing your shoulder blades together. 

Lower the band and stand tall, then perform a face pull by driving your elbows upward and outward, bringing your hands toward your face. 

Return to the starting position and repeat continuously.

exercises.name: SuperBand Seated Face Pull
exercises.description:
1) While seated on the floor with legs straight out in front of you, hold the handles.

2) Place the centre of the band around both of your feet.

3) Sit tall with abs tight and hold handles in front of you.

4) Pull the handles back until they are up next to your forehead. 

5) Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Seated Row
exercises.description:
1) While seated on the floor with legs straight out in front of you, hold the handles.

2) Place the centre of the band around both of your feet.

3) Sit tall with abs tight and hold handles in front of you with elbows bent next to your side.

4) Pull the handles back until they are next to your side and elbows are behind you.

5) Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright placing feet inside the band, should be shoulder-width apart, toes pointed. Hands holding both sides of the band at shoulder level, elbows bent and out

2.Lift the bands up until arms are fully extended overhead.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Side Lunge
exercises.description:
Anchor one end of a superband at ground level. Loop the other end around the foot you will step out with. 

Stand with both feet together, core engaged. Step the banded foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, keeping your chest upright and the other leg straight. 

Push through the lunging leg to return to the starting position, then repeat the movement continuously with the same leg.

exercises.name: Superband Side Plank Row
exercises.description:
Anchor one end of a superband to a stable object near the floor. Get into a side plank position on your forearm, keeping your body in a straight line and your legs staggered for stability. 

Hold the free end of the band in your top hand. While maintaining the side plank, perform a row by pulling the band toward your torso, driving your elbow upward and squeezing your shoulder blade. 

Lower your hand back to the starting position and repeat

exercises.name: Superband Side Step to Press
exercises.description:
Stand on a superband with both feet shoulder-width apart, holding the band handles at shoulder height. 

Step laterally to one side, keeping tension in the band and maintaining a slight bend in your knees. As you complete the step, press the band overhead in a controlled motion. 

Lower back to shoulder height, step laterally in the opposite direction, and repeat continuously, maintaining tension in the band throughout.

exercises.name: SuperBand Single Arm Lat Pull Down
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a seated position, grasp the band with one hand. 

3. Fully extend your arm up towards the anchor point, until your elbow is straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until the handle is next to your shoulders. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: SuperBand Single Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing inside the loop with a single leg and holding the band with the same side hand. 

2. Raise the band up and away from the body until your arm is parallel with the floor. 

3. Slowly return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.
